name: CN/IC EMPLOYEES CREDIT UNION
description_short: Memphis-based credit union serving employees of CN Railroad, Illinois Central Railroad, and Amtrak since 1932. Offers savings, loans, mobile banking, and retirement accounts.
description_long:
CN/IC Employees Credit Union was established in 1932 to serve a specific, defined membership: employees of CN Railroad, Illinois Central Railroad, and Amtrak. The credit union operates from its headquarters in Memphis, Tennessee, and maintains a member-owned, cooperative structure typical of credit unions. Over nine decades, it has positioned itself as a specialized financial institution for railroad employees seeking accessible banking services.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of standard banking services including regular savings accounts (minimum $25), certificate accounts (6/12/24-month terms, minimum $500), and multiple IRA account types (Traditional, Roth, Coverdell Educational). Members can access loans, credit and debit cards, travel and gift cards, and share drafts (checking) accounts. The institution provides mobile banking through the CNICECU app available on iOS, Android, and iPad, enabling balance viewing, fund transfers, loan payments, and transaction searches. Shared branching and ATM access are available, along with miscellaneous financial services.

What distinguishes CN/IC Employees Credit Union is its highly specialized field of membership—limited to railroad industry employees and their families. This focused membership base allows the credit union to tailor products specifically for its members and maintain deep community relationships within the railroad employee community. The institution emphasizes member ownership and control, explicitly stating that the credit union is "owned and controlled by its members." Their slogan "All Aboard the Savings Train" reflects their railroad heritage and brand identity.

The primary limitation is accessibility: membership is restricted to employees of specific railroad companies and their families. Website content is dated (deposit rates last updated 04/01/2019), which raises questions about current rate competitiveness and service evolution. Operating hours are limited to Monday-Friday, 9:00 a.m.–4:30 p.m. CST, which may not accommodate members with non-traditional schedules. The institution appears to prioritize stability and traditional banking over digital innovation, though mobile banking is offered.
pros: Membership restriction ensures a tight-knit community focused on railroad employees' specific financial needs
Low savings account minimum ($25) makes membership accessible to members starting their savings journey
Multiple retirement account options (Traditional, Roth, Coverdell Educational IRAs) with 12-month terms
Mobile app (CNICECU) available across iOS, Android, and iPad for convenient account management
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Established 1932 presence provides stability and 90+ years of institutional knowledge
Shared branching and ATM access extends beyond the single Memphis location
cons: Severely restricted field of membership—only employees of CN Railroad, Illinois Central Railroad, and Amtrak qualify; applicants must verify employer eligibility
Deposit rates last updated April 1, 2019, raising concerns about current competitiveness and whether rates are actively maintained
Limited operating hours (Monday-Friday, 9:00 a.m.–4:30 p.m. CST) with no weekend or evening availability
Savings accounts limited to 6 withdrawals per month without fee, which may frustrate frequent savers
Website appears outdated with copyright year of 2026 and minimal modernization visible in design or content
best_for: Employees of CN Railroad, Illinois Central Railroad, or Amtrak seeking stable, member-owned banking
Railroad industry workers interested in IRAs and retirement planning with personalized service
Members prioritizing community-focused banking over digital-first fintech solutions
Long-term savers with funds designated for specific goals (education, vacation, emergency reserves)
diagnosis: CN/IC Employees Credit Union is best for current or former employees of CN Railroad, Illinois Central Railroad, or Amtrak who value member ownership, personalized service, and traditional banking products over digital innovation. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is strictly gatekept to specific railroad employers—those outside these companies cannot join, making it inaccessible to the general public despite offering solid financial products.
services: Regular Share (Savings) Accounts with $25 minimum and quarterly dividend payments
Certificate Share (Savings) Accounts with 6, 12, or 24-month terms and $500 minimum
Traditional IRA accounts with $500 minimum and 12-month terms
Roth IRA accounts with $500 minimum and 12-month terms
Coverdell Educational IRA accounts for education savings
Personal Loans with online application available
Mobile Banking App (CNICECU) for iOS, Android, and iPad
Shared Branching and ATM access network
